brief: The Global Hiring Playbook — 11-section resource lead-gen page (generative STRESS TEST). Copy verbatim from copy-brief.md; every section exercises a catalog device event-genlaunch did not (split art-panel hero w/ meta, stats flow, interlock, navy-well card grid, deep-accent split accordion w/ media swap, info-band comparison rows, inset-glow banner, edge-cut testimonial carousel, badge+rating group strips, navy non-exclusive FAQ, tel/textarea form on the closing noise band).

sections.note: STRESS 1 — split art-panel hero WITH media counterweight PLUS the poster's eyebrow+meta registers: the sanctioned inset noise panel, left content stack, right product-UI collage. Distinct from the homepage hero (globe, no eyebrow/meta) and from event-genlaunch's centered solo poster.

copy.heading: Hire in any country before your competitors open the tab

copy.text: Twelve chapters on employing, contracting, and paying people in 170+ countries — written by the legal and payroll teams who run it for real, not summarized from search results. Free, and updated every quarter.
12 chapters · 40+ templates · Free · Updated quarterly

asset.alt: Remote product UI showing a global team roster with local employment details

slots.name: background
slots.role: inset rounded panel w/ noise-gradient art

treatments.note: the brand's rounded inset panel on its own noise-gradient art; ink text on it

sections.note: STRESS 2 — stats band through the generic-flow module fold: four metric modules (value as caption register, label as paragraph). Probes the missing `stat` big-number renderer; page must still read well.

copy.text: Every country rewrites the rules — employment law, payroll cadence, benefits, termination. The playbook exists so your first hire abroad doesn't become your first legal project abroad.
Countries where the playbook's guidance is grounded in Remote's own local entities.

copy.heading: 6–12 months
copy.text: Typical time to open a local entity yourself — the path the playbook helps you skip.

copy.heading: 90 days
copy.text: How often employment law changes somewhere you might hire — and how often we update.
What the playbook costs. The expertise is the product demo.

sections.note: STRESS 3 — ordinary evidence-grounded media split: complete caption/statement/support/action stack at left, landscape transparent illustration at right. The content shape intentionally does not invoke the advanced interlock device.

copy.text: FROM THE FIELD

copy.heading: Hiring in a new country should feel like hiring across the street.

copy.text: That's the standard the playbook holds every chapter to — if a step can't be done in an afternoon, we show you the infrastructure that makes it one.

asset.alt: Globe illustration with orbiting Remote product-UI chips

sections.note: STRESS 4 — the brand's workflow-card grammar (features-card-grid-navy-media): three flat white cards with deep-navy media wells, card eyebrows, gridEqualize heights, curated centered standalone header. Event-genlaunch used bento, never this grid.

copy.heading: Twelve chapters. Three you'll open first.

copy.text: Each chapter pairs plain-language guidance with the templates and checklists our own teams use — copy them, adapt them, ship them.

slots.name: cards
slots.role: module — chapter cards w/ navy media wells

copy.heading: Hire full-time anywhere, without an entity
copy.text: When employer of record beats opening an entity, how to read an EOR contract, and the questions to ask about who really employs your people.
copy.alt: Remote EOR product UI in a deep-navy media well

copy.heading: Work with contractors without the misclassification bill
copy.text: The tests countries actually apply, contract templates that hold up locally, and when a contractor is legally already your employee.
copy.alt: Remote contractor management product UI in a deep-navy media well

copy.heading: Run multi-country payroll people can set their watch to
copy.text: Payroll calendars, currencies, and statutory payments across every market you operate in — and what "consolidated payroll" should really mean.
copy.alt: Remote global payroll product UI in a deep-navy media well

sections.note: STRESS 5 — the brand's signature deep-accent split accordion (feature-accordion-deep-accent): four process steps, ACTIVE step inverts to the deep-accent inset card with circle-arrow affordance, per-step media swap (rowMedia) in the square counterweight well. Event-genlaunch's disclosures ran through the FAQ composer, never this split device. The page's single accent deployment.

copy.heading: From "can we hire there?" to a signed offer

copy.text: Four steps, in the order real teams take them. Open a step to see the chapter and template that carries it.

slots.name: list
slots.role: list — accordion steps w/ deep-accent active state

copy.label: Scope the country
copy.text: Chapter 2's country scorecards: employment cost, required benefits, notice periods, and the local quirks that surprise finance teams — before you promise anyone a start date.

copy.label: Choose the employment model
copy.text: Entity, EOR, or contractor — chapter 4's decision tree prices each path against your timeline and headcount, in plain numbers.

copy.label: Make the offer compliant
copy.text: Localized offer letters and benefits baselines from chapter 7, drafted from the playbooks our own legal teams maintain in every market.

copy.label: Run the first payroll
copy.text: Chapter 10's first-90-days checklist: payroll registration, statutory filings, and the calendar that keeps payday boring.

asset.alt: Remote product UI for the active process step

sections.note: STRESS 6 — build-vs-buy comparison as the info-band split's ruled label/value rows beside the infrastructure proof UI. Probes the missing comparison-`table` contract renderer (blocks.yaml declares `table`; no composer draws one).

copy.heading: Opening an entity vs. hiring through Remote

copy.label: Time to first hire
copy.value: Own entity: 6–12 months of registration and setup. Through Remote: days, on infrastructure that already exists.

copy.label: Upfront cost
copy.value: Own entity: incorporation, local counsel, capital requirements. Through Remote: a per-employee price you can read.

copy.label: Ongoing compliance
copy.value: Own entity: your team tracks every law change. Through Remote: our local teams do — it's the same infrastructure this playbook is written from.

copy.label: Exit
copy.value: Own entity: winding down takes as long as setting up. Through Remote: offboard compliantly and close the line item.

copy.text: Chapter four runs this comparison for the twelve countries teams ask about most — with the real numbers filled in.

asset.alt: Remote infrastructure product UI with compliance status cards

sections.note: STRESS 7 — the captured inline conversion banner (cta-inline-banner): rounded inset panel with the soft tinted glow, question-form heading, exactly ONE filled pill. A compact conversion beat, not a closing section.

copy.heading: Ready to make your next country the easy one?

sections.note: STRESS 8 — the captured testimonial carousel (testimonial-card-row): 3-up white quote cards with circle avatars + name/role person rows, EDGE-CUT presentation (cut-at-viewport carousel track), closing outlined pill. Captured quotes verbatim; real captured avatars.

sections.note: STRESS 9 — the captured third-party-validation strip (badge-award-strip): six G2 award shields as one measured strip row, three review-platform rating chips as a second, ONE outlined pill. Exercises the generic-flow multi-GROUP mark rows (badges + ratings render as separate strips, never merged). All badge/rating art is real captured assets.

copy.heading: The #1 global HR platform as voted by you

sections.note: STRESS 10 — FAQ disclosure, NAVY-INVERSE variant: knobs.faq.activeSurface = surface/inverse (the brand's deep-navy well surface as the active card — the accent inversion is spent on pb-process), exclusive:false (multiple items may be open — unexercised knob), first item open at rest, warm-wash idle hover.

copy.heading: What to know about the playbook

copy.question: Is it really free?
copy.answer: Yes. No paywall, no trial. You give us a work email; we give you twelve chapters and every template. That's the whole exchange.

copy.question: Who writes it?
copy.answer: The same in-house legal, payroll, and mobility teams who run employment for Remote's customers in 170+ countries. Not freelancers, not aggregated blog posts.

copy.question: How current is the guidance?
copy.answer: Every chapter is reviewed quarterly, and material law changes ship as updates in between. The playbook you download today is the playbook our own teams used this morning.

copy.question: Is it useful if we never buy Remote?
copy.answer: Yes, deliberately. The scorecards, templates, and decision trees work whichever path you take — the playbook argues for doing it right, not for doing it with us.

copy.question: What formats does it come in?
copy.answer: Web and PDF, chapter by chapter or the whole thing. Templates arrive as editable documents your team can adapt.

sections.note: STRESS 11 — compact resource form on the brand's closing noise-gradient band (cta-closing-noise): white form panel riding the art. Exercises the `tel` field kind (never used) and a deliberate `textarea` kind the schema does NOT support — expected coercion to a single-line text input (logged as a design-system gap).

copy.text: Twelve chapters, 40+ templates, updated quarterly — free. Tell us where to send it and where you're hiring next, and chapter one is in your inbox in about a minute.

copy.consent: By downloading, you agree to Remote's Terms and Privacy Policy. We'll email you the playbook and its updates — unsubscribe anytime.
copy.success: It's on its way. Check your inbox for chapter one and your template library.
